weapons would presage a devastating war leading to utter confu-
sion, in the near future.
[Here the commentator raises an objection : Is not the author,
who has already referred to the blazing of Praharana or weapon in
verse 19 supra, guilty of repetition ? He says that actually there is
no repetition. For, according to Nagnajit there are' three kinds of
Ayudhas or weapons viz. Praharana, Pftnimukta (those that are
thrown by the hand), and Tantramukia (those flung by mechanical
device). The first category includes weapons like swords; the
second, like discuses; and the third, like stones, arrows, missiles,
rods etc. The word Ayudha is all-inclusive, wheres ana, restrict-
ive, meaning only swords, axes etc. Hence there is no fault com-
mitted by our respected author. Though there is no difference

380 Bfhat Samhifa
in the effect, yet the author has mentioned the two words separately
